solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int rangeBitwiseAnd(int left, int right) {
        int shift = 0;
        while (left < right) {
            left >>= 1;
            right >>= 1;
            ++shift;
        }
        return left << shift;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The algorithm shifts both left and right to the right until they are equal, effectively finding the common prefix of their binary representations. The result is then formed by shifting the common prefix back to the left. This approach is efficient and avoids iterating through the entire range.>
